notes:
E2B (e2b.dev) sells secure sandboxed 'computers' for AI agents. Signup goes through a Supabase-backed dashboard at /auth/sign-up — email/password or GitHub OAuth, no public registration REST endpoint. An AI agent without a browser cannot click through. Expected Tier 3.

Contract scope: signup attempts autonomous registration via agent_task (expected to FAIL — documents the blocker honestly). Persistence + workflow probe the public API health endpoint at https://api.e2b.dev/health, which returns 'Health check successful' without auth — that gives us a 'service is alive' signal independent of signup autonomy.

If E2B ever ships a POST /api/v1/register-agent (or equivalent) with no human gate, rewrite signup to call it and this becomes Tier 1.

agent_task.prompt: Attempt to sign up autonomously for E2B (https://e2b.dev). E2B offers sandboxed 'computers' for AI agents. Probe every public API/auth surface you can discover — the docs at https://e2b.dev/docs, /api endpoints on api.e2b.dev, /auth routes — and attempt to create an account + obtain an API key WITHOUT a browser-based dashboard step. Do NOT use any pre-provisioned env credentials. If autonomous signup is genuinely impossible (dashboard OAuth, email/password form with Supabase Auth, CAPTCHA), report that conclusion explicitly and do NOT fabricate an api_key. Never invent a credential.

assertions.description: agent produced an E2B API key autonomously (EXPECTED TO FAIL — E2B requires a Supabase-backed dashboard signup)

steps.description: GET /health on api.e2b.dev — unauthenticated liveness probe. Returns 'Health check successful'.

assertions.description: E2B API is reachable (service is alive)

steps.description: Re-probe /health to confirm API stability. Without autonomous credentials we cannot test the core sandbox workflow (spawn/exec/terminate).

assertions.description: API remains reachable across the run
